courses.subject: INFD
courses.title: Infectious Diseases in Public Health Practice

meetings.instructor:
Professor LEE Shui Shan, 
Professor WONG Ngai Sze

Professor LEE Shui Shan, 
Professor WONG Ngai Sze

courses.description: This course is designed to develop basic knowledgebase in infectious diseases in the society, in contexts of causation, impacts, diagnosis and epidemiology.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For TPg students in Master of Public Health / Postgraduate Diploma in Public Health
courses.learning_outcomes:
Students are expected to
1) Have a basic familiarity with major infectious disease pathogens
2) Know the differences between viruses, bacteria, fungi, and parasites
3) Understand how the human immune system protects against infection
4) Be introduced to infectious diseases of public health significance
courses.course_syllabus:
| 1. Overview of microbiology, immunology, diagnostics. 2. Emerging Infectious diseases |
| --- |
| Infectious diseases of public health importance part 1 |
| Infectious diseases of public health importance part 2 |
courses.required_readings: Please refer to the course outline provided by course teacher
courses.recommended_readings:
Public Health Infectious Disease by Prof SS Lee.

ISBN 9789881594815

courses.subject: INFD
courses.title: Infection Control in Health Care and Community Settings

meetings.instructor:
Dr. ZEE Sze Tsing Jonpaul, 
Professor CHEN Hong, 
Professor CHOI Kin Wing, 
Professor FUNG Sau Chun Kitty, 
Professor MA Siu Keung, 
Professor YUEN Pak Leung, 
Professor YUNG Wai Hung Raymond

courses.description: Students would be introduced to the principles and practice of infection control in three settings–hospital and clinic, laboratory and the community, through a series of lectures tailored to the needs of public health professionals. The emphasis would be on the development of a systematic approach to infection control practice.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in Master of Public Health
courses.learning_outcomes:
Student should be able to:
1. update on the principles of infection control in health care setting, laboratory and community
2. familiarize with system development in infection control practice
courses.course_syllabus:
| L1: Disinfection and sterilization |
| --- |
| L2: Standard precautions and transmission based precautions |
| L3: (I) Immunity and immunization and (II) Vaccination of health care workers |
| L4: Infection control in (I) special hosts and (II) community and special environment |
| L5: Outbreak management (I) case finding and control measures; (II) Decision making in infection control |
| L6: Control of airborne infections in hospital and institutional environments Post-exposure prophylaxis |
| L7: Surveillance in infection control (I) programme design and data collection; (II) data analysis and presentation |
| *L8: Antimicrobial resistance (I) mechanism; (II) containment programmes* |
| Root Cause Analysis |
| L9: Engineering design for isolation and operation theatre |
courses.required_readings: Please refer to the course outline provided by course teacher
courses.recommended_readings:
| Hospital Epidemiology and Infection Control. C. Glen Mayhall, ed. Philadelphia. Wolters Kluwer Health/Lippincott Williams & Wilkins. C 2012, 4th Ed. CHP and HA Isolation Precautions guidelines http://www.chp.gov.hk/files/pdf/isolation\_precautions.pdf Control of transmission of TB in healthcare settings in the Hospital Authority http://www.chp.gov.hk/files/pdf/transmission\_tb\_20051103.pdf WHO Policy on Tuberculosis Infection Control in Health-Care Facilities http://whqlibdoc.who.int/publications/2009/9789241598323\_eng.pdf Recommendations on the Postexposure Management and Prophylaxis of Needlestick Injury or Mucosal Contact to HBV, HCV and HIV http://www.chp.gov.hk/files/pdf/recommendations\_on\_postexposure\_management\_and\_prophylaxis\_of\_needlestick\_injury\_or \_mucosal\_contact\_to\_hbv\_hcv\_and\_hiv\_eng\_r.pdf |
| --- |

courses.subject: INFD
courses.title: Public Health and Infectious Disease

meetings.instructor:
Professor MOK Ka Pun, 
Professor TUN Hein Min

Professor MOK Ka Pun, 
Professor TUN Hein Min

Professor MOK Ka Pun, 
Professor TUN Hein Min

courses.description: This course aims to bridge virological and bacteriological concepts with laboratory-based public health practices, focusing on the impact of infectious diseases.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in Master of Public Health
courses.learning_outcomes:
Students should be able to:

1. Master basic knowledge of microbiology and immunology

2. Discuss vaccinology in depth

3. Describe Global programmes for:

• Respiratory and gastrointestinal infections

• Vaccine access

• Antimicrobial resistance

• Emerging infectious disease

4. Access and appraise information about infectious diseases
courses.course_syllabus:
| Microbiology review (Microbes and the Body) |
| --- |
| Water- and food- borne diseases |
| Influenza Viruses |
| Coronaviruses |
| Viral Hemorrhagic Fevers |
| Mid-term examination |
| Antimicrobial Resistance |
| Zoonosis and One Health |
| Advanced Vaccinology |
| Biosafety and Techniques in Infectious Diseases |
| Global Disease Control Programs and Policies |
| Presentations |
| Final examination |
courses.recommended_readings:
Public Health Infectious Disease by Lee Shui-shan

Publisher: Stanley Ho Centre for Emerging Infectious Diseases
